fundamentals.name: iShares Russell 2000 ETF
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The fund generally invests at least 80% of its assets in the component securities of its underlying index and in investments that have economic characteristics that are substantially identical to the component securities of its underlying index (i.e., depositary receipts representing securities of the underlying index) and may invest up to 20% of its assets in certain futures, options and swap contracts, cash and cash equivalents.

news.paragraph: The iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM) is being highlighted as a potential investment radar item, coinciding with significant market commentary from Paul Tudor Jones regarding an $8 billion bet on small-cap chaos. Additionally, the ETF's performance is being discussed in the context of dollar strength and geopolitical uncertainty surrounding an Iran deal, suggesting potential shifts in sector performance. These developments point to a complex environment for small-cap equities.

headlines.title: Should iShares Russell 2000 ETF (IWM) Be on Your Investing Radar?
Paul Tudor Jones makes $8 billion bet on small-cap chaos
Dollar Holds Firm Amid Iran Deal Uncertainty: ETFs to Win/ Lose
